title: Tweet by @KanikaBK: Cursor open-sourced Priompt, their JSX-based context-priority prompt library
snippet: Cursor just open-sourced the internal prompt library that powers their $9B code editor. It's called Priompt. JSX-based, assign priorities to every part of your prompt — low priority content drops when context fills up. Scopes, isolation, and inspection built in.
summary: Cursor open-sourced Priompt, a JSX-based prompt library that manages context window pressure by letting you assign priorities to prompt components — low-priority content is automatically dropped when the context fills up. Has scopes, isolation, and inspection built in; battle-tested inside the Cursor editor. Minor — open-source engineering tool, not an open-weight model or local inference research artifact.

title: Tweet by @techNmak: 9-layer production AI architecture breakdown
snippet: services/ RAG pipeline, semantic cache, memory, query rewriter, router. agents/ document grader, decomposer, adaptive router. prompts/ versioned, typed, registered. security/ input, content, output guards. evaluation/ golden dataset, offline eval, online monitor. observability/ per-stage tracing.
summary: A breakdown of a 9-layer production AI architecture covering services (RAG, caching, routing), agents (grading, decomposition), versioned prompts, multi-stage security, evaluation, and observability layers. Engineering best-practice pattern rather than a specific tool or paper. Minor.

title: Tweet by @arceyul: GitHub's spec-kit — specification-first AI coding workflow
snippet: GitHub published spec-kit (95k stars, 8.3k forks, MIT). Six commands convert an idea into a structured spec before writing code: /speckit.constitution, /speckit.specify, /speckit.clarify, /speckit.plan, /speckit.tasks, /speckit.implement. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot and 25+ agents.
summary: GitHub open-sourced spec-kit, a tool that enforces specification-first AI coding by converting informal ideas into structured specs before any code is written. Six slash commands walk through constitution, specification, clarification, planning, task ordering, and implementation. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot and many other AI coding assistants. Minor — tooling workflow, not an open-weight model or inference technique.

title: Tweet by @xiaoying_eth: 10 AI agent GitHub repos worth bookmarking
snippet: OpenHands (SWE-bench 77.6%), Hermes Agent (190k stars, self-training), CrewAI (multi-agent collaboration), Aider (terminal AI pair programmer), n8n (400+ service automation), LangGraph (production agent scheduling), Cloudflare Agentic Inbox, Browser Use (99k stars), awesome-mcp-servers, claude-task-master.
summary: Curated list of 10 open-source AI agent tools: OpenHands autonomous coder (77.6% SWE-bench), Hermes Agent self-training personal AI, CrewAI multi-agent framework, Aider terminal pair programmer, n8n self-hosted automation, LangGraph state-graph agent orchestrator, Cloudflare Agentic Inbox, Browser Use browser automation, awesome-mcp-servers directory, and claude-task-master multi-agent coordinator. Wiki-relevant — several tools worth individual pages.

title: Tweet by @askalphaxiv: Latent Thought Flow — GFlowNet distribution over reasoning paths
snippet: Latent Thought Flow moves reasoning into continuous latent space, learning a distribution over many paths via a continuous GFlowNet. Gives more probability to trajectories that are correct and cheap; stops early on easy problems, thinks longer on hard ones. Better accuracy with reduced reasoning length.
summary: Latent Thought Flow is a research technique that models reasoning as a distribution over multiple latent paths using a continuous GFlowNet, rather than a single fixed trajectory. It assigns higher probability to correct and efficient paths, enabling adaptive computation per-problem. Achieved better accuracy with reduced reasoning length. Wiki-relevant research paper — arXiv ID not confirmed, save for follow-up.
